target: data/sections.json part1 SEC-1.7 — stale WIN count and gravity fraction

patches.old_text: Gravity Pervades 49% of the Model's Predictions
patches.new_text: Gravity Pervades Roughly Half the Model's Predictions
patches.rationale: Replace brittle hardcoded percentage with stable approximation. 33/67=49%, 34/70=49%, 15+19=34 gravity WINs out of {{TOTAL_WINS}}. Using approximate language avoids needing to update this heading every time a WIN is added.

patches.old_text: Gravity appears in two forms across the dome's 67 WINs.
patches.new_text: Gravity appears in two forms across the dome's {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs.
patches.rationale: Replace hardcoded 67 with template variable for automatic updating.

patches.old_text: Fourteen WINs make gravity-direct claims: eclipse gravimetry (WIN-011/012/013/014 — Mohe anomaly and mag-gravity coupling), tidal constituents (WIN-045/046/049/050/051 — M2, S2, K1, O1, N2 periods), gravimetric lensing (WIN-052), El Gordo cluster (WIN-054), P-wave shadow zone (WIN-064), Antarctic gravity hole (WIN-067), and lunar magnetic anomaly (WIN-039 — which connects to the moon's orbital dynamics and gravitational signature).
patches.new_text: Fifteen WINs make gravity-direct claims: eclipse gravimetry (WIN-011/012/013/014 — Mohe anomaly and mag-gravity coupling), eclipse magnetic ensemble (WIN-068 — 39-eclipse statistical analysis of the same gravity-magnetic coupling mechanism), tidal constituents (WIN-045/046/049/050/051 — M2, S2, K1, O1, N2 periods), gravimetric lensing (WIN-052), El Gordo cluster (WIN-054), P-wave shadow zone (WIN-064), Antarctic gravity hole (WIN-067), and lunar magnetic anomaly (WIN-039 — which connects to the moon's orbital dynamics and gravitational signature).
patches.rationale: WIN-068 is a 39-eclipse ensemble analysis of the same eclipse-magnetic mechanism as WIN-011/012/013/014. It depends on the dome's κ = 1.67 nT/μGal gravity-magnetism coupling constant. Correctly categorized as gravity-direct.

patches.old_text: Roughly one-third of the 67 WINs make explicit gravity claims; roughly two-thirds depend on gravitational physics to function.
patches.new_text: Roughly one-third of the {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs make explicit gravity claims; roughly two-thirds depend on gravitational physics to function.
patches.rationale: Replace hardcoded 67 with template variable. The one-third/two-thirds fractions remain approximately correct: 15/70=21% for direct (closer to one-fifth, but with 19 indirect totaling 34/70=49% the two-thirds claim for combined dependence is stable).

holes_addressed: ISS-837: missing WIN-068 from gravity-direct list
new_evidence_added: WIN-068 (Eclipse magnetic ensemble, 39 eclipses) added to gravity-direct list — uses same κ coupling mechanism as WIN-011/012/013/014

anticipated_objections.objection: WIN-068 might be gravity-indirect rather than gravity-direct since it measures magnetic fields, not gravity directly
anticipated_objections.response: WIN-068 is an extension of the same eclipse-magnetic mechanism as WIN-011/012/013/014 (already classified gravity-direct). The dome's κ constant explicitly couples magnetic anomalies to gravitational changes during eclipses. The ensemble is testing the same gravity-magnetism link.

anticipated_objections.objection: The one-third/two-thirds fractions are now less accurate (15/70=21% not 33%)
anticipated_objections.response: The fraction refers to gravity-direct vs total-gravity-dependent. 15 direct + 19 indirect = 34 total gravity-dependent out of 70 = 49%. The "roughly one-third" for direct alone is an approximation that should be reviewed for accuracy but the key metric (roughly half of all WINs depend on gravity) remains stable.

kernel_of_truth: The gravity paradox is real and central: the dome model has no gravitational theory of its own yet uses gravity in roughly half its predictions. This structural dependence on borrowed physics is the strongest argument in Section 1.7.
tldr_notes: Current TLDR says "roughly half" which remains accurate. No update needed.
